pattern: A window has word2 as a rearrangeable prefix exactly when its letter counts dominate word2's counts, so slide a window tracking how many of the 26 letter-requirements are still unmet, and every further extension to the right of a satisfying window is valid too. The larger word1 bound here just forces the O(n) two-pointer version instead of anything quadratic.
hints: "Can be rearranged to have word2 as a prefix" only depends on letter counts: a substring works iff, for every letter, it contains at least as many copies as word2 does.

Use a two-pointer window: for each left endpoint, find the smallest right endpoint where the window's letter counts finally cover word2's counts -- every larger right endpoint from there on also works.

Track how many of word2's distinct letters are still under-satisfied in the window; when that count hits zero the window (and everything to its right) is valid, so add (n - right) to the total and shrink from the left.

edge_inputs.description: word2 longer than word1, must short-circuit to zero
word1 never contains one of the needed letters
requirement needs repeated letters, not just distinct ones
word2 length is one less than word1's
word2 is a single repeated letter

stress.description: near-linear-limit word1 with a small alphabet, proving the O(n) window scales
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 200000))
    word1 = ''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(n))
    m = rng.randint(1, 6)
    word2 = ''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(m))
    return [word1, word2]
